evals.prompt: Write a workflow for the code review process that includes all the detailed steps and examples.
evals.expected_output: Apply workflow writing rules: (1) Goal in 1 sentence. (2) Steps as imperative commands. (3) Output as template headers only — no pre-filled rows. No prose explanations. No pre-answered questions. No example data in tables. Merge any sequential steps that always happen together. Stay under 80 lines.

evals.prompt: The workflow I created has detailed explanation paragraphs before each step explaining why we do it.
evals.expected_output: Violation: No verbose step preambles. Delete all 'Before we start, it's important to understand...' prose. Replace with imperative command: 'Run security scan' not 'Before running the security scan, it's important to note that...'. If a bullet achieves the same result, delete the paragraph. Check: would cutting this in half still give the agent enough to act on?

evals.prompt: Update this SKILL.md — it has an inline code block of 15 lines showing an example configuration.
evals.expected_output: Violation: Inline code block >10 lines must be extracted to references/. Create references/example.md (or references/config-example.md) and move the 15-line block there. Replace inline block with a link: 'See [Configuration Example](references/config-example.md)'. SKILL.md must stay ≤100 lines.

should_not_trigger: Build a REST API endpoint for user registration
Write SQL queries for a reporting dashboard
